subject: Packet-header 81-byte invariant + version byte + nonce/sender binding
rationale: All five falsifier tests pass. The codec encodes the canonical 1-byte version + 8-byte nonce + 4-byte srcEid + 32-byte sender + 4-byte dstEid + 32-byte receiver (total 81 bytes per `messagelib/contracts/uln/ReceiveUlnBase.sol:_assertHeader`). Distinct (nonce, sender) tuples produce distinct keccak256(header) hashes. Two OApp distinc-the-same-bucket nonce path is correctly keyed.
scope_remaining: Reachable engine-level signal that could move to production-grade measurement: a forged DVN-side `verify(...)` overwrite of `confirmations` (downgrade via same-dvn-required-plus-optional). Source-pinned canonical `ReceiveUlnBase._verify` overwrites via `mapping(...)=Verification(true, _confirmations)`. If the same DVN is simultaneously required and optional, a single lower-call-by-malicious-DVN could, in principle, lower the confirmations from a higher earlier call. Requires DVN cooperation + identical (sender, eid) trajectory.
next_phase_input: Transfer to Phase 2A or close honest-zero per Phase-1 decision gate (see summary.json).
